rulings.Case Title: MARILOU D SAMSON VS. RUTH CUSING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Fo Roder Re: Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Prosecution [Ccp 1008]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013, Line 28, PLAINTIFF MARILOU SAMSON's Motion For Reconsideration of order Re: Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Prosecution [Ccp 1008]. Denied. Ms. Samson has neither presented new facts or new law that warrants reconsideration. (CCP § 1008.) Neither Ms. Samson's difficulty with her representation by counsel nor the defendants' default is a ground for tolling the five year period to bring an action to trial. (CCP § 583.340.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK MALKASSIAN et al VS. HOWARD MULLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Howard R. Mullin, Individually And Dba As Trustee Of The Mullin Family Trust Dated Oct 1, 1982; Susan Mullin, Individually And Dba As Trustee Of The Mullin Family Trust Dated Oct 1, 1982'S Motion For An Order To Compel The Deposition Of The Person(S) Most Knowledgeable Of Brps Llc And Production Of Documents Thereat And For Monetary Sanctions Against Brps Llc And Its Attorneys Of Record, Shannon B. Jones Law Group, In The Amount Of $2500
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013, Line 25, Defendant Howard R. Mullin, Individually And Dba As Trustee Of The Mullin Family Trust Dated Oct 1, 1982; Susan Mullin, Individually And Dba As Trustee Of The Mullin Family Trust Dated Oct 1, 1982'S Motion For An Order To Compel The Deposition Of The Person(S) Most Knowledgeable Of Brps Llc And Production Of Documents Thereat And For Monetary Sanctions Against Brps Llc And Its Attorneys Of Record, Shannon B. Jones Law Group, In The Amount Of $2500. Granted. Within 15 days of this order, BRPS, LLC shall produce its person most knowledgeable for deposition and produce documents. In their email correspondence, the parties agreed to an extension of the discovery cut off date, including this motion to compel the deposition of BRPS, LLC's person most knowledgeable and the production of documents. The extension was reciprocal of the discovery deadlines for both parties and benefitted both parties. BRPS, LLC is a party to the instant action, and the Mullins are entitled to take the deposition of BRPS, LLC's person most knowledgeable. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CATHY EILENBERGER UBELL et al VS. WILKES BASHFORD 375 SUTTER STREET, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST ELEVATOR CORPORATION DBA AMTECH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court denies the motion for summary judgment/adjudication. Defendant maintained its initial summary judgment burden by relying on plaintiff's factually devoid special interrogatory responses. In opposition, the declaration of Mr. Gerk is admissible to establish what qualifies as due diligence and what customary duties defendant owed. Plaintiff creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached its duty and acted negligently. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CARTER et al VS. PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE GROUP LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Tuesday, July 9, 2013, Line 4: Defendants BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT INC.'S, BRPS LLC'S (formerly known as GMAC HOME SERVICES, LLC), and REAL LIVING REAL ESTATE, LLC'S Motion for Protective Order Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KARMA QUICK VS. JONATHAN FRIED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT JONATHAN FRIED's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. The amended complaint sufficiently pleads the terms of the contract, plaintiff's performance, defendant's breach, and damages. There is nothing wrong to referring to the EDD proceedings and defendant cannot demurrer to an allegation. The retaliation causes of action sufficiently allege protected activity. Paragraph 7 sufficiently alleges PAGA exhaustion. Even if the Court takes judicial notice of the 7/5/12 letter, it does not demonstrate a failure to exhaust. Labor 2699.3(b)(2)(A)(iv) provides: "The superior court shall not dismiss the action for nonmaterial differences in facts or theories between those contained in the notice of violation provided to the division and employer pursuant to paragraph (1) and the complaint filed with the court." The citation to Villacres v. ABM Industries Inc. (2010) 189 Cal.App.4th 562, is off point. That was not a pleading case and a party cannot demurrer to a claim for relief. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts showing that the misclassification was willful. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To A Request For The Production Of Docuiments-Set One; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defts And Their Attorney Jonathan Fried;
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Tuesday, July 9, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff JOHN STEVENS' Motion to Compel Further Response to a Request for the Production of Documents - Set One; Request for Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants and Their Attorney Jonathan Fried Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ASHLEY GLENNEN VS. ALLERGAN, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT ALLERGAN, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of exhibit 1 (PMA letter). The FDA required defendant to provide training to physicians. Pars. 19 and 20 of the 2nd AC demonstrate that the training was provided. To the extent plaintiff is alleging that the training was insufficient and something more was necessary, federal law expressly preempts the claim. To the extent plaintiff relies on violations of federal regulations without citing a state-law duty that parallels a federal-law duty, the claim is impliedly preempted. See Stengel v. Medtronic, Inc. 704 F.3d 1224, 1230 (9th Cir. 2013). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN TERENZIO et al VS. CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTs CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, CURRENT MEDIA, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION & AL GORE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the first and third causes of action. Sustained, without leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff TAP has not pled any facts to allege damages from Defendants' purported actions. Plaintiffs have not pled any facts to allege the personal liability of Mr. Gore for Plaintiffs' alleged dealings with Defendants Current TV and Current Media. As pled, Mr. Terenzio alleges that he intended to broker the sale of a business opportunity that is within the purview of Bus. & Prof. Code §§ 10130, 10131(a). (Complaint, 16, 17, 25.) Plaintiffs have not alleged any facts to show that Mr. Blum was Defendants' agent. Leave to amend is granted, if they can do so in good faith, for Plaintiffs to distinguish their proposed involvement in the sale of Current TV and to allege estoppel based on theories of ostensible authority and ratification. For the second cause of action, unjust enrichment is not a cause of action. (Melchior v. New Line Productions, Inc. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 779, 793.) For the third cause of action, Plaintiffs have not alleged that Defendants' consent or that Defendants requested Mr. Terenzio's services, but rather that Mr. Terenzio presented his unsolicited proposal to Defendants. (Complaint, 12, 13.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
